risks.title: Runtime adapter complexity may be underestimated
risks.scenario: Codex and Claude Code expose different session, permission, and transcript mechanics, making a single adapter interface leaky.
risks.why_current_artifacts_do_not_prevent_it: The semantic contract defines adapter responsibilities but not backend-specific failure modes.
risks.required_probe_or_change: M1 must start with one backend and treat the second backend as an adapter compatibility test.

risks.title: Manual lint may give false confidence
risks.scenario: The artifact set passes current checks, but future edits break INDEX truthfulness or hash consistency if updates remain manual.
risks.why_current_artifacts_do_not_prevent_it: The checks are command-backed but not packaged as a repeatable tool yet.
risks.required_probe_or_change: M0 should include a small semantic-artifact lint script before large artifact growth.

risks.title: Semantic roles could drift into process roles
risks.scenario: Implementation names modules after roles and accidentally gives each role a permanent process or worktree.
risks.why_current_artifacts_do_not_prevent_it: The spec states the boundary, but implementation naming can still blur it.
risks.required_probe_or_change: Implementation architecture must include a RoleAgent versus RuntimeSession module boundary.

ambiguities.consequence: Adapter details and test harnesses diverge.
ambiguities.required_resolution: Resolve during implementation_architecture, not before semantic approval.

recommended_next_action: Proceed, but make adapter selection and executable linting first-class in the next design step.
